ncs_standard_definition.description_short: An Agricultural Data Scientist collects, analyzes, and interprets large datasets to optimize crop yields, reduce waste, and improve agricultural productivity.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Agricultural Data Scientists work with farmers, researchers, and policymakers to develop data-driven solutions for sustainable agriculture. They design and implement data collection systems, develop predictive models, and create visualizations to communicate insights to stakeholders.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.Tech in Computer Science or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture and data science can pursue a degree in Computer Science or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can bridge into Agricultural Data Science by acquiring relevant certifications and skills.

future_builder.description: Agricultural professionals looking to advance in their careers can develop advanced skills in data science and machine learning.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience in agriculture or a related field

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into Agricultural Data Science by acquiring relevant skills and building a portfolio.
portfolio_proof_required: Machine learning model for crop yield prediction

roadmap.title: Capstone and Portfolio
topics: Machine Learning Model for Crop Yield Prediction
